The director needs to arrange the scenes in a sequence where no two consecutive scenes are from the same decade. The release years are 1994, 2004, 2007, 2012, and 2015 which correspond to the decades {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, {2000-2009}, {2010-2019}, and {2010-2019} respectively. The shooting days required are 4, 6, 8, 10, and 12 days. The goal is to minimize the total number of shooting days by arranging the scenes in a way that avoids consecutive scenes from the same decade. One possible optimal sequence is 1994, 2004, 2012, 2007, 2015 which corresponds to the decades {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, {2010-2019}, {2000-2009}, {2010-2019} and results in total shooting days of 4+6+10+8+12=40 days. Another possible sequence is 1994, 2007, 2012, 2004, 2015 which corresponds to the decades {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, {2010-2019}, {2000-2009}, {2010-2019} and results in total shooting days of 4+8+10+6+12=40 days. Both sequences achieve the same total shooting days of 40 days. ******

The director has a budget of ₹50 lakhs and the cost per day for each scene is ₹1 lakh, ₹1.5 lakhs, ₹2 lakhs, ₹2.5 lakhs, and ₹3 lakhs respectively.
